informal: Let $p$ be an odd prime. Show that $a$ is a primitive root modulo $p$ iff $a^{(p-1) / q} \not \equiv 1(p)$ for all prime divisors $q$ of $p-1$.
lean:
theorem Ireland_Rosen_exercise_4_8_corrected {p : ℕ} (hp : Odd p) (hp' : p.Prime) (a : (ZMod p)ˣ) :
  IsPrimitiveRoot (a : ZMod p) (p - 1) ↔
    ∀ q : ℕ, q ∣ (p - 1) → q.Prime →
      ¬ ((a : ZMod p) ^ ((p - 1) / q) = 1)
reasoning: The Lean statement encodes "a is a primitive root mod p" as `IsPrimitiveRoot (a : ZMod p) (p - 1)`, which matches because the multiplicative group of ZMod p has order p−1 when p is prime. Taking `a : (ZMod p)ˣ` reflects the implicit assumption gcd(a,p)=1 in the informal statement. The right-hand side quantifies over natural numbers q with both `q ∣ (p−1)` and `q.Prime`, which is precisely "for all prime divisors q of p−1", and asserts a^((p−1)/q) ≠ 1 in ZMod p. The hypotheses `Odd p` and `p.Prime` correctly capture "odd prime p". The biconditional matches the informal "iff", so the formalization is logically equivalent.
